# Cookey
> Source: https://github.com/Lakr233/Cookey
> Site: https://cookey.sh

Cookey captures authenticated browser sessions from an iPhone and delivers
them encrypted to a CLI terminal as Playwright-compatible storageState files.

## Install
Download the latest binary from GitHub Releases:
  https://github.com/Lakr233/Cookey/releases/latest

Assets:
  cookey-macOS.zip          macOS universal (arm64 + x86_64), signed & notarized
  cookey-linux-x86_64.zip   Linux x86_64
  cookey-linux-aarch64.zip  Linux aarch64
  skills.md                 Tool description file for LLM agents

## Commands
  cookey login <url>       Start session capture (prints QR code in terminal)
  cookey status [rid]      Check session status
  cookey export <rid>      Export Playwright storageState.json to stdout
  cookey list              List all sessions and daemons
  cookey rm <rid>          Delete a session
  cookey config            Manage defaults (server, transport, timeout)
  cookey server            Run a self-hosted relay server

## Workflow
  cookey login https://example.com/login
  # → QR code appears in terminal
  # → User scans with Cookey iPhone app, logs in
  # → Encrypted session delivered to CLI
  cookey export <rid> > storageState.json
  # → Use with Playwright, Puppeteer, or any automation tool

## Architecture
- End-to-end encrypted (X25519 + XSalsa20-Poly1305)
- Zero-knowledge relay server (never sees plaintext)
- Self-hostable via Docker (Server/Dockerfile)
- No accounts or registration required
